architecture.summary: ForestGPT is a domain-specialized LLM assistant for forestry professionals, built on a four-level development roadmap: (1) pre-training a transformer on curated forestry literature for baseline conversational capability; (2) augmenting with Retrieval-Augmented Generation to ground answers in local and time-sensitive documents; (3) coupling growth simulators for scenario modeling; and (4) integrating sensor, drone, and machinery data streams for real-time decision support. A Level-1 prototype was deployed via mobile app at Futa Expo 2025, demonstrating lightweight fine-tuning on open-weight checkpoints while maintaining human-in-the-loop oversight.
trained_components: transformer pre-trained on curated forestry literature

biblio.title: ForestGPT and Beyond: A Trustworthy Domain-Specific Large Language Model Paving the Way to Forestry 5.0

claims.statement: A Level-1 prototype successfully guided multilingual visitors and demonstrated the feasibility of lightweight fine-tuning on open-weight checkpoints
Generic large language models are limited by generic training data and propensity for hallucination, restricting reliability in safety-critical fields like forestry
The proposed four-level development path addresses two looming risks: unverified adoption of generic outputs and professional mistrust of opaque algorithms
Retrieval-Augmented Generation augmentation grounds answers in local and time-sensitive documents to improve reliability
Coupling growth simulators enables scenario modeling for forestry decision support
Integrating continuous streams from sensors, drones and machinery provides real-time decision support data
Trustworthy, explainable, and accessible LLMs with human-in-the-loop guardrails can accelerate transition to Forestry 5.0
Technical challenges analysed include multimodal grounding, continual learning, safety certification, data sovereignty, bias, and change management
